text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: for a field array whose resolver can report both a root-level error on the array itself (e.g. "needs at least N items") and nested per-index errors on individual items in the same validation pass, calling trigger() alongside a field-array action such as remove() can wipe out the nested per-index errors, leaving only (or none of) the root error behind.

Concretely: set up a form with useFieldArray where the resolver returns an errors object shaped like { test: { type: 'min', message: '...', 0: {...}, 2: {...} } } — a plain object that carries the root error's type/message directly on itself while also carrying nested errors keyed by numeric index (this is the shape useFieldArray's own internal effect leaves errors in after an array mutation). Trigger validation once — both the root error and the nested per-index errors render correctly. Now call a field-array mutator like remove(i) followed immediately by trigger('test') again. After this second validation pass, the previously-visible nested per-index error(s) disappear from formState.errors, even though the resolver is still reporting them and the indices have simply shifted after the removal.

Fix the library source so that when an existing field-array error is already a plain object carrying nested numeric-indexed errors (not a bare root-only error, and not already an array), it is not coerced/wrapped in a way that hides those nested indices — the root error info and the nested per-index errors must both remain independently readable from formState.errors after subsequent trigger() calls. Do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run the relevant tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useFieldArray.test.tsx`. Fix the library source; do not just edit tests.

text: I’ll trace the field-array error normalization and trigger/update path, then add a focused regression test only if the existing suite does not already capture the failure. I’ll keep the fix in library source and run the specified Jest target.

text: The likely collision point is `updateFieldArrayRootError`, which is used both during field-array mutations and resolver-driven updates. I’m reading its callers and the existing root-error tests now to verify where the plain object is being transformed and what shape downstream code expects.

src/utils/cloneObject.ts:19:  if (!isArray && !(isObject(data) && isPlainObject(data))) {
import isObject from './isObject';

export default (tempObject: object) => {
  const prototypeCopy =
    tempObject.constructor && tempObject.constructor.prototype;

  return (
    isObject(prototypeCopy) && prototypeCopy.hasOwnProperty('isPrototypeOf')
  );
};

text: The failure mechanism is confirmed: `updateFieldArrayRootError` always converts an existing non-array value into a one-element array, so a hybrid object such as `{ type, message, 0, 2 }` loses direct numeric access when reused on the next trigger. The fix needs to preserve that hybrid object while still wrapping bare root-only resolver errors and preserving normal array errors.

/bin/bash -lc 'git show 6067c3f8''^:src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts 2>/dev/null || true; git show 6067c3f8 -- src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts src/logic/createFormControl.ts'

text: I’ll update the shared helper so it reuses an existing non-array object only when it has at least one numeric key; bare root-only objects continue to be wrapped, and arrays remain untouched. I’ll also add a regression covering `remove()` followed by `trigger('test')`, including shifted indices and simultaneous root/nested access.

text:
Fixed `src/logic/updateFieldArrayRootError.ts`.

Existing hybrid field-array error objects with numeric nested keys are now preserved instead of being wrapped into an array. Bare root errors and existing arrays retain their previous behavior.

Verified:

- `useFieldArray.test.tsx`: 72 tests passed
- TypeScript check passed
- `git diff --check` passed
